metadata.proof_point: 87% reduced pull-up use in 1 month, Internal survey, N=5,679

copy.preview: Whether it's a glance down or a step toward the bathroom, that's real progress. Here's the next size.

sections.name: OPENING, Universal Recognition (R1, R3, R4)
sections.content:
Whether your child is 3 or 10, verbal or non-verbal, diagnosed or still figuring it out, 90 days of body-signal learning is real time.

Some kids show their first pause within weeks. Others take months before the nervous system registers anything new. Both are normal. (R5, Timeline Flexibility)

You bought the 3+3 bundle 90 days ago. Let's check in.

---

sections.name: RECOGNITION ANCHORS, The Progress Check (R2, R6)
sections.content:
You might have noticed something, or maybe nothing obvious yet. Here are the signals parents tell me about around the 90-day mark:

, Whether your child says "wet" or shows you with their body, the pause before an accident is real. Even once. *(R4, Verbal/Non-Verbal Inclusive)*

, They pull at their pants instead of sitting in a wet pull-up without flinching. *(R6, Low-Bar Success Metric)*

, Look down at the spot after it happens, because for the first time, they *felt* something. *(R6)*

, Stay dry 2 hours instead of 30 minutes. Or 4 hours instead of 1. *(R6, any range is a win)*

, Walk toward the bathroom, even after an accident, because some part of the connection is forming. *(R6)*

, And if none of these have happened yet: that does not mean nothing is happening. Some nervous systems take longer to build the pathway. *(R5, Timeline Flexibility)*

---

### THREE-FAMILY TESTIMONIAL PATTERN, Real Parents at 90 Days (GF Inclusion)

Every child's 90-day mark looks different. Here's what three parents shared about theirs:

"He went from 'almost trained' to 'mostly' in about 6 weeks. At 3 months, he was initiating, coming to tell me. I didn't think that would ever happen."
, Sarah's mom, Level 1

"Week 10 was when she finally paused. Just paused. She was playing and she stopped and looked down. That had never happened. We're not there yet, but she's *feeling* something now."
, Lisa's mom, Level 2

"He's not trained. He may never be fully trained in the way I hoped. But last week, for the first time in 9 years, he looked at the wet spot. Not with distress. Just notice. That's enough for now."
, Maria's mom, Level 3

These three different kids, three different timelines, one common thread: the body-signal pathway takes time, and the first signal looks different for every child.

---

sections.name: MECHANISM, Why 90 Days Is a Milestone (Mechanism 50%)
sections.content:
Ninety days of Body-Signal Learning Layer exposure means the nervous system that picks up signals slowly has had sustained, gentle feedback, 30 to 60 seconds of sensation per wear, to start building a connection that never existed before.

The science is clear: body signal differences in autism are real and measurable (Barmpagiannis & Baldimtsi 2025, 49-study systematic review). The internal "I need to go" signal doesn't arrive the way traditional methods assume. Pull-ups wick away the sensation entirely, the brain gets nothing to work with. The Body-Signal Learning Layer provides the feedback that pull-ups remove.

Some brains need 2 weeks to register the signal. Others need 12 weeks or more. Both are within the normal range for autistic children. *(R5, Timeline Flexibility)*

---

### UTILITY, Ready for the Next Size? (CTA 30%)

At 90 days, two things often happen at once:

1. Your child may be ready to size up, the 3+3 bundle fits best when you have the right sizes in rotation.
2. The 3+3 pairs have been through 90 days of wear and wash cycles. Fresh pairs mean consistent feedback.

CTA: Re-order your 3+3, or size up if your child has grown.

> Re-Order Your 3+3 Bundle → [Link]

*Or if you're not sure about sizing: reply to this email and tell me about your child. I'll help you figure out the right fit.*

---

sections.name: GUARANTEE REMINDER
sections.content:
Every pair comes with the 60-day guarantee. Always has. If these aren't working for your child, full refund. No questions.

---

### LENA SIGN-OFF (MEDIUM Variant)

I have two autistic sons. One's 6, still in the middle of it. The other took us until he was almost 8. I know what 90 days of waiting for something to happen feels like, and what it feels like when that first signal finally arrives.

Wherever you are at this milestone: there's no deadline here. Just keep going at your child's pace.

Lena —,

*P.S. Hit reply if you've seen any new signals, even tiny ones. I read every response.*

---

# PART 2, FULL CREATIVE STRATEGY

## Big Idea

"The 90-Day Nervous System Check-In." Not a sales reminder, a milestone marker. 90 days is the point where nervous system that picks up signals slowlys have had sustained, repeated feedback from the Body-Signal Learning Layer. This email celebrates whatever signal arrived (or didn't yet) while opening the door to sizing up. The tone is functional, warm, wholly realistic, no hype, no pressure, just a parent-to-parent check-in with a re-order option attached.

| Element | Calibration |
|---------|-------------|
| Emotional Arc | Acknowledge milestone → Recognize progress (any level) → Normalize varied timelines → Offer next step |
| Hope/Realism | 60/40, Optimistic but grounded. Early-mini-wins celebrated without overpromising. |
| Energy | Calm, warm, functional. "Helpful reminder" energy. |
| Voice Variant | V1(W) Warm · V8(R) Reminder · V14 Realistic |
| Lena Position | Peer-mom who has lived 90-day milestones |
